options: A: Add godAdapter+reviewerAdapter optional props to StatusBar
B: Do not modify StatusBar
rationale: Card A.1 explicitly requires StatusBar God display when different from reviewer; optional props preserve backward compat

options: A: Export sub-components for direct unit testing
B: Test only via full wizard flow
rationale: ink-testing-library stdin escape sequence handling prevents reliable multi-step wizard flow testing; exporting GodSelector and ConfirmScreen enables focused unit tests
residual_risk: exported internals increase public API surface slightly

options: A: god field uses config.god (from user selection)
B: god field hardcoded to config.reviewer (original code)
rationale: Original code had bug: onConfirm always set god=reviewer, ignoring user's GodSelector choice. Fixed to use config.god which is set during select-god phase.

options: A: Add TASK_INIT state between IDLE and CODING
B: Handle TASK_INIT in App.tsx useEffect without state machine change
rationale: Card A.2 explicitly requires adding TASK_INIT state to XState. Option A follows AR-003 (XState preserved, God as async effect handler)
residual_risk: Existing tests needed updating from START_TASK→CODING to START_TASK→TASK_INIT→CODING

options: A: Add sendStartAndSkipInit helper function
B: Manually update each test individually
rationale: Helper function reduces diff size and clearly communicates intent; all existing tests that dont test TASK_INIT behavior can use the helper

options: A: Use DegradationManager with TASK_INIT_SKIP on failure
B: Try-catch only with direct CODING fallback
rationale: DegradationManager tracks consecutive failures and can disable God at L4 (AR-004). Using it here ensures consistent degradation behavior across all God call points

options: A: Render TaskAnalysisCard as overlay within MainLayout
B: Render TaskAnalysisCard as full replacement for MainLayout while active
rationale: Rendering as full replacement avoids useInput conflicts between TaskAnalysisCard and MainLayout keyboard handlers. Card is shown briefly (max 8s) so no loss of MainLayout features.
residual_risk: Messages added during TASK_INIT are not visible while card shows; they appear after card dismissal

options: A: Send TASK_INIT_COMPLETE immediately after God analysis, show card during CODING
B: Defer TASK_INIT_COMPLETE until card confirmed, show card during TASK_INIT state
rationale: Deferring TASK_INIT_COMPLETE keeps the state machine in TASK_INIT during card display, which is semantically correct - we haven't finished task initialization until user confirms. Also allows user to change taskType before CODING starts.

options: A: Async IIFE pattern (same as TASK_INIT)
B: Keep synchronous v1 path, only God path async
rationale: Consistent async pattern with TASK_INIT useEffect. Both paths (God available / God disabled) handled cleanly with cancellation support.

options: A: On God throw, fallback to v1 decidePostCodeRoute in same useEffect
B: On God throw, send PROCESS_ERROR and let xstate handle
rationale: AR-004 requires old components as fallback. Falling back to v1 in the same useEffect is transparent to the state machine — it still receives the same events (ROUTE_TO_REVIEW etc). PROCESS_ERROR would disrupt the workflow unnecessarily.

options: A: Async IIFE pattern (same as ROUTING_POST_CODE)
B: Keep synchronous v1 path, only God path async
rationale: Consistent async pattern with ROUTING_POST_CODE (B.1) and TASK_INIT useEffects. Both paths (God available / God disabled) handled cleanly with cancellation support.

options: A: On God throw, fallback to v1 decidePostReviewRoute in same useEffect
B: On God throw, send PROCESS_ERROR and let xstate handle
rationale: AR-004 requires old components as fallback. Falling back to v1 in the same useEffect is transparent to the state machine — it still receives the same events (ROUTE_TO_EVALUATE, ROUTE_TO_CODER, CONVERGED). PROCESS_ERROR would disrupt the workflow unnecessarily.

options: A: Async IIFE pattern (same as ROUTING_POST_CODE/ROUTING_POST_REVIEW)
B: Keep synchronous v1 path, only God path async
rationale: Consistent async pattern with ROUTING_POST_CODE (B.1) and ROUTING_POST_REVIEW (B.2) useEffects. Both paths (God available / God disabled) handled cleanly with cancellation support.

options: A: On God throw, fallback to v1 convergenceRef.current.evaluate() in same useEffect
B: On God throw, send PROCESS_ERROR and let xstate handle
rationale: AR-004 requires old components as fallback. Falling back to v1 in the same useEffect is transparent to the state machine — it still receives the same events (CONVERGED/NOT_CONVERGED). PROCESS_ERROR would disrupt the workflow unnecessarily.

options: A: God prompt replaces ContextManager.buildCoderPrompt when available
B: Always use ContextManager, God only supplements
rationale: FR-003 explicitly states God replaces ContextManager prompt building. choiceRoute still takes precedence for interrupt/choice paths. Fallback to v1 when God degraded or taskAnalysis null (AR-004).

options: A: God prompt replaces ContextManager.buildReviewerPrompt when available
B: Always use ContextManager, God only supplements
rationale: Consistent with CODING useEffect pattern. FR-003 requires God to replace ContextManager for both Coder and Reviewer prompts. v1 fallback preserved (AR-004).

options: A: Render banner as overlay within MainLayout
B: Render banner as full replacement for MainLayout while active
rationale: Consistent with TaskAnalysisCard pattern. Full replacement avoids useInput conflicts between GodDecisionBanner Space/Esc handlers and MainLayout keyboard handlers.
residual_risk: Messages added during banner display are not visible until banner dismissal

options: A: 1-second tick intervals (like TaskAnalysisCard)
B: 100ms tick intervals for smooth progress bar
rationale: FR-008 specifies a 2-second window which is very short. 100ms ticks provide smooth visual feedback during this brief period, unlike TaskAnalysisCard which has 8 seconds.
residual_risk: Slightly more frequent re-renders, negligible for 2s duration

options: A: Show banner for request_human decisions
B: Skip banner, go directly to manual mode
rationale: request_human means God itself defers to human. Showing a 2s banner for this would be counterproductive—it adds delay before the user can interact, defeating the purpose of request_human.

options: A: Unified withGodFallback helper wrapping all God call points with retry
B: Keep ad-hoc degradation handling in each useEffect
rationale: Card C.2 explicitly requires unified withGodFallback wrapper. Pure function in god-fallback.ts is testable and consistent. withGodFallbackSync variant handles prompt generation.
residual_risk: Type narrowing after withGodFallback requires type assertions since God and v1 return different types

options: A: Include degradationState in both saveState calls (transition + Ctrl+C exit)
B: Only persist on explicit exit
rationale: Both saveState calls must include degradationState to ensure resume always has current state. SessionState already has the field defined.

options: A: Render overlay as full replacement for MainLayout
B: Render overlay within MainLayout as sub-overlay
rationale: Consistent with TaskAnalysisCard and GodDecisionBanner patterns. Full replacement avoids useInput conflicts between ReclassifyOverlay arrow/enter/esc handlers and MainLayout keyboard handlers.
residual_risk: Messages added during overlay display are not visible until overlay dismissal

options: A: Add reclassify action to processKeybinding and route via MainLayout onReclassify
B: Handle Ctrl+R directly in App.tsx useInput
rationale: Consistent with existing keybinding architecture (Ctrl+C→interrupt, Ctrl+G→god overlay). processKeybinding is the single source of truth for keyboard mapping.

options: A: Reuse GodDecisionBanner component directly
B: Create separate PhaseTransitionBanner with same pattern
rationale: PhaseTransitionBanner has different props (nextPhaseId, previousPhaseSummary) and different visual treatment (magenta border, phase-specific text). Separate component avoids overloading GodDecisionBanner with dual-purpose logic while maintaining the same 2s countdown pattern.
residual_risk: Slight code duplication in countdown/progress bar logic

options: A: Interrupt LLM before showing overlay when in CODING/REVIEWING
B: Show overlay without interrupting, interrupt only on confirm
rationale: Card spec explicitly states: "正在运行 LLM 时，先 interrupt 再显示 overlay". Interrupting first ensures clean state transition and prevents useInput conflicts between running LLM output handling and overlay keyboard input.
residual_risk: LLM work in progress is lost on Ctrl+R during CODING/REVIEWING

options: A: Save godAdapter, godTaskAnalysis, godConvergenceLog in both transition and Ctrl+C save calls
B: Only save in transition save, skip Ctrl+C
rationale: Both save points must include God fields to ensure duo resume always has current state, regardless of exit method (normal transition vs Ctrl+C).

options: A: Initialize from restoredRuntime.godConvergenceLog
B: Always start empty, rebuild from history
rationale: ConvergenceLog entries are God-specific metadata not derivable from history.jsonl. Must be restored from snapshot to maintain continuity.

options: A: Initialize useState from restoredRuntime.godTaskAnalysis
B: Re-run TASK_INIT on resume to get fresh analysis
rationale: FR-011 requires restoring taskAnalysis from snapshot. Re-running TASK_INIT would waste a God API call and change task classification. The original analysis from session start is the source of truth.

options: A: Test at XState + God module level (no React rendering)
B: Test with full React rendering via ink-testing-library
rationale: Integration tests verify the interaction between XState state machine and God modules (task-init, god-router, god-convergence, auto-decision, phase-transition, degradation-manager, god-fallback). React rendering is tested separately in existing ui/ tests. Module-level integration avoids complex ink stdin mocking while testing the actual God workflow logic.
residual_risk: React-level wiring (useEffect dispatching events) is tested separately in existing ui/ tests, not duplicated here

options: A: Write StatusBar unit tests + verify existing audit coverage is complete
B: Add redundant audit calls at App.tsx success paths (already handled by God modules internally)
rationale: StatusBar rendering and handleLog implementation were completed in prior cards (A.1, B.4, C.2). God modules (god-router, god-convergence, auto-decision) already call appendAuditLog internally on success paths. App.tsx only needs failure/degradation audit entries. Card D.2 scope is tests + verification.
